definitions.definition: p-Block elements are those elements which belong to groups 13, 14, 15, 16 and 17 of the periodic table. These elements are called p-block elements because they are located in the p-block of the periodic table.
Group 15 elements are also known as nitrogen family. The elements of group 15 are N, P, As, Sb and Bi.
definitions.example: Example: Phosphorus (P) is a non-metal and a member of group 15.

definitions.definition: Group 16 elements are also known as chalcogens. The elements of group 16 are O, S, Se, Te and Po.
definitions.example: Example: Oxygen (O) is a non-metal and a member of group 16.

definitions.definition: Group 17 elements are also known as halogens. The elements of group 17 are F, Cl, Br, I and At.
definitions.example: Example: Chlorine (Cl) is a non-metal and a member of group 17.

definitions.definition: Group 18 elements are also known as noble gases. The elements of group 18 are He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe and Rn.
definitions.example: Example: Argon (Ar) is a noble gas and a member of group 18.

definitions.definition: Nitrogen family is another name for group 15 elements.
definitions.example: Example: Nitrogen (N) is a non-metal and a member of the nitrogen family.

definitions.definition: Chalcogens are another name for group 16 elements.
definitions.example: Example: Oxygen (O) is a non-metal and a member of the chalcogens.

definitions.definition: Halogens are another name for group 17 elements.
definitions.example: Example: Chlorine (Cl) is a non-metal and a member of the halogens.

definitions.definition: Noble gases are another name for group 18 elements.
definitions.example: Example: Argon (Ar) is a noble gas and a member of the noble gases.

definitions.definition: Valence shell is the outermost shell of an atom which contains the valence electrons.
Valence electrons are the electrons present in the valence shell of an atom.
p-Block is the right-hand side block of the periodic table which contains the p-block elements.
Atomic radius is the distance between the nucleus and the outermost electron present in an atom.
Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract a shared pair of electrons towards itself in a covalent bond.
Hofmann bromide test is used to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary amines.
Rosenmund's reaction is used to prepare acetyl chloride.
Alkyl halides are the organic compounds which contain a halogen atom bonded to a carbon atom.
Alkyl halide is a type of organic compound which contains a halogen atom bonded to a carbon atom.
Aryl halides are the organic compounds which contain a halogen atom bonded to an aromatic ring.
